programme.title: Spitfire
programme.desc: The story of the British single-seat fighter plane the Supermarine Spitfire, designed by RJ Mitchell and famous for its role in the Battle of Britain. Archive footage, new film of the aircraft in flight and interviews with the last surviving veterans who flew the plane in combat combine to tell its story.

programme.title: Lancaster
programme.desc: The story of the legendary bomber synonymous with the Dambusters, the night raids against Germany and the brave men and women who made them possible.

programme.title: Williams & Mansell: Red 5
programme.desc: Documentary charting the rise of Nigel Mansell, the Williams F1 racing team and how they came together to fight for the Formula 1 World Drivers' Championship.

programme.title: Hill
programme.desc: A profile of ex-Formula One driver Damon Hill, revealing how he overcame the adversity of losing his father Graham to follow in his footsteps and become world champion himself.

programme.title: Premier League Legends
programme.desc: This episode looks back on the career of Arsenal legend Ian Wright. The former England man scored 113 Premier League goals during his top flight career with Arsenal and West Ham.

programme.title: Discovering: Sean Penn
programme.desc: A profile of the American actor and director, who starred in Dead Man Walking, Carlito's Way and The Thin Red Line and won Academy Awards for his roles in Mystic River and Milk.

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming tours the less-celebrated sights of Brighton, beginning by visiting the Toy and Model Museum to look at its collection of model trains. He then discovers a boutique that sells a variety of unusual items, before taking a glimpse behind the scenes at the Royal Pavilion.

programme.title: Discovering: Jean Harlow
programme.desc: The return of the programme shining a light on stars from the golden age of Hollywood, beginning with a look at the life and career of 1930s sex symbol Jean Harlow.

programme.title: The Nineties
programme.desc: The concluding part of the examination of the decade's most memorable TV shows, from South Park and Seinfeld to The Sopranos and Friends.

programme.title: All That Breathes
programme.desc: Amidst the darkening backdrop of Delhi's apocalyptic air and escalating violence, two brothers devote their lives to protect one casualty of the turbulent times - the bird known as the Black Kite. Documentary, starring Salik Rehman, Mohammad Saud and Nadeem Shehzad.

programme.title: Dogtown and Z-Boys
programme.desc: Documentary charting the history of skateboarding, from its 1970s emergence as a land-based form of surfing in Los Angeles and its subsequent explosion of popularity worldwide, to its current status as a widely-practised extreme sport. Narrated by Sean Penn, with contributions from experts Jay Adams and Tony Alva.

programme.desc: The England netball international talks about her idol.

programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?
programme.desc: Investigating the unsolved 2015 murder of John Palmer, who was nicknamed Goldfinger after his involvement in melting down gold from the 1983 Brink's-Mat robbery. Palmer was found dead in his Essex garden and while police originally believed he died of natural causes, they eventually confirmed the case was being treated as a professional hit.

programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?
programme.desc: The search for John Palmer's killer leads to Tenerife, where he built a timeshare empire. Undercover stings and Russian mob ties expose the scale of his power.

programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?
programme.desc: A professional hitman is suspected in John Palmer's murder, with evidence pointing to a cartel and an Eastern European enforcer.

programme.title: Chernobyl: The Lost Tapes
programme.desc: Newly discovered archive footage and witness accounts reveal the story behind the 1986 nuclear disaster and the heroic efforts made to prevent another explosion.

programme.title: 2000 Meters to Andriivka
programme.desc: Documentary following a Ukrainian platoon as they traverse a heavily fortified mile of forest to liberate a strategic village from Russian forces.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: The final country on the Mekong's journey is Vietnam, the home of the river's mighty delta. This area is a true biological treasure trove.

programme.title: Wildlife Icons
programme.desc: A look at meerkats, which may be small and vulnerable, but what they lack in size, they make up for in tenacity.

programme.title: Gangs of Baboon Falls
programme.desc: As the Zimbabwe and Zambia gangs negotiate predators while foraging on the river banks, the Victoria gang try to keep a new food source hidden from baboons and humans alike.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: The powers of fire and ice collide in Iceland, actually Earth's largest volcanic island. Take a journey to this country driven by the forces of nature.

programme.title: Extreme Africa
programme.desc: The Simien Mountains National Park in Ethiopia, home to some of the rarest creatures on Earth. This film offers a glimpse into life on some of the highest peaks in Africa, in the last stronghold of shaggy, grass-grazing geladas, elusive Simien wolves and horned walia ibex.

programme.title: Wild Philippines
programme.desc: Discovering a world shaped by fire and water, as cameras capture Philippine eagles soaring above ancient forests and rare crocodiles lurking below the river's surface.

programme.title: Great Blue Wild
programme.desc: The local economy of Palau relies heavily on eco-tourism. But as this documentary shows, the country is fighting a hard battle against man-made environmental destruction.

programme.title: Wild Dogs: Pack vs Pride
programme.desc: Following the wild dog packs of Africa. Roaming with the pups of the Manzi and Kakumbi packs in South Luangwa, Zambia. Snowy faces a hyena before Weka must search for her offspring.

programme.title: Wild Dogs: Pack vs Pride
programme.desc: Julz runs into a lion-hyena showdown and Henry goes on a house call to the Kakumbi den, where a pup has gone AWOL.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: A return to Iceland to see how its landscapes are changing through the retreat of its glaciers. Every year, their tremendous power alters the nation for ever.

programme.desc: Documentary following daily life at Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre in Dorset, where staff look after monkeys, chimps, orangutans and other primates.

programme.desc: Julz tracks the Kakuli pack and a Manzi faction that has broken away. The Kakumbi make a kill before a happy Manzi reunion.

programme.desc: Julz and Henry track the Kakumbi pups as they face off with a razor-tusked warthog and observe the prides pushing the Manzi from their territory.

programme.desc: Chip and Jo meet with a client who just purchased a 1950s bungalow hoping to make it her forever home after losing her husband to cancer.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: After experiencing a devastating tornado, a family wants to build an off-grid retreat overlooking Tennessee's Ocoee River; as amateur builders, they must rely on the help of friends and family, and each other, to reach the finish line.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: Off-the-grid contractor Gregg Smith battles the elements to build a cliff-side retreat for a family in the Sierra Nevada Mountains; for Gregg and his team, erecting this house in the middle of nowhere comes with its share of challenges.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: From the edge of an active volcano in Hawaii and the Appalachian Mountains to the deserts of New Mexico and upstate New York, builders battle harsh conditions to construct innovative, self-sufficient homes in remote areas.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: An outdoor enthusiast works to build his dream retirement home in the Idaho mountains, but weather and material shortages slow his timeline.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: These builds bring new meaning to the term middle of nowhere, taking place high on top a sub-zero glacier, miles out in the middle of the Atlantic, deep in Alaska's untamed wilderness, and on a secluded beach off the coast of Belize.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: A unique pre-Civil War cabin is taken apart and restored, highlighting the pioneer craftmanship while adding a contemporary flair.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark Bowe reveals how "Barnwood Builders" saved a two-story log cabin in New Freeport, Pennsylvania; after saving every log possible, they travel to Albertville, Alabama, to rebuild a home on the edge of a cliff.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark looks back on some of the most creative ways the team has transformed cabins and barns; from repurposing leftover material to restoring a cabin that looks beyond repair, Mark can always dream up new ways to give old wood a new life.

programme.title: Big Sky Kitchen With Eduardo Garcia
programme.desc: Montana-based chef Eduardo Garcia invites his buddies over for some backyard archery practice, followed by a campfire cookout that includes paella, grilled tomatoes, and homemade lavender-honey ice cream.

programme.title: Big Sky Kitchen With Eduardo Garcia
programme.desc: Montana-based Chef Eduardo Garcia spends the day in the kitchen perfecting his Reuben sandwich recipe; it starts with smoked pastrami, homemade sauerkraut and ends with 1000 island dressing and cheese that's piled high on rye bread.

programme.title: Big Sky Kitchen With Eduardo Garcia
programme.desc: Chef Eduardo Garcia prepares handmade pappardelle pasta and lamb shank for Becca and Goose.

programme.title: Big Sky Kitchen With Eduardo Garcia
programme.desc: It's taco night and friends are coming to dinner; Chef Eduardo Garcia presses tortillas for tostadas, slow-cooks creamy black beans with pork belly, grills a showstopping lobster Aguachile, and drizzles churros with goat caramel for all to share.

programme.title: Big Sky Kitchen With Eduardo Garcia
programme.desc: Chef Eduardo Garcia brings his sister's crew over for a campfire lunch, featuring a homemade game stock French onion soup topped with griddled swiss, comte and gruyere croutons, as well as campfire crepes with a huckleberry sauce.

programme.title: Big Sky Kitchen With Eduardo Garcia
programme.desc: A hearty breakfast at camp is in order before a day on horseback; Chef Eduardo Garcia greets the day and saddles up by the fire, coal-roasting Beet and Sweet Potato hash, and baking Square Bale Biscuits with a creamy Cowboy Hollandaise sauce.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: Mark and Mary are looking for a lake house that can serve as a meeting place for their large family; they customize an Ohio lakefront home for their large family.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: Lisa and Angela are co-workers and friends that are searching for a house with the help of their daughters Daniele and Allie, and are hoping to find the perfect vacation home to share between their two growing families.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: After spending years living all over the world, Teresa and Jeremy find the perfect vacation home for their family in Texas.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: New parents look to escape their busy New York City lives with a move to Wolf Lake, a small, rustic lake community in upstate New York; the properties they consider require significant renovations and may break the bank.
